mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS remains below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance when rates are near zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 10 percent, it signals a severe labor market contraction that forces corporate earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 10.0, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: In an acute supply shock regime, a declining industrial production index reflects capacity constraints that suppress corporate output, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of observations during the supply shock regime, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year consumer price index increase exceeding 2.5 percent triggers expectations of central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5 percent, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate payrolls, which directly supports earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with payroll changes.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where quarterly GDP growth remains positive while retail sales decline indicates structural demand weakness that suppresses consumer-facing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal predicts negative returns in less than 40 percent of cases, or the joint model fails to outperform a baseline model using only GDPC1 in out-of-sample forecasting.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rebounds from a trough while the federal funds rate remains near zero, liquidity conditions amplify the supply recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility in out-of-sample testing.
